metadata.description: 模式匹配 (match): Python 3.10+ supports the `match/case` pattern with various modes including literal values, wildcards, sequence unpacking, and guard conditions. Here's a brief Python code example demonstrating some of these features: ```python def handle_value(value): match value: case 42: # Literal value print("The answer to everything!") case _ as x if isinstance(x, int) and x > 100: # Guard condition with wildcard print(f"Value is an integer greater than 100: {x}") case [a, b]: # Sequence unpacking print(f"Parsed a list: {a}, {b}") case _: # Default case print("Unknown value") # Example usage: handle_value(42) # The answer to everything! handle_value(101) # Value is an integer greater than 100: 101 handle_value([1, 2]) # Parsed a list: 1, 2 handle_value("hello") # Unknown value ``` This example demonstrates using literal values (`42`), guard conditions with wildcards ( `_ as x if ...` ), sequence unpacking (`[a, b]`), and a default case (`_`).

data_pointers.title: 模式匹配 (match)
data_pointers.content_preview: 模式匹配 (match): Python 3.10+ supports the `match/case` pattern with various modes including literal values, wildcards, sequence unpacking, and guard conditions. Here's a brief Python code example demonstrating some of these features: ```python def handle_value(value): match value: case 42: # Literal value print("The answer to everything!") case _ as x if isinstance(x, int) and x > 100: # Guard condition with wildcard print(f"Value is an integer greater than 100: {x}") case [a, b]: # Sequence unpacking print(f"Parsed a list: {a}, {b}") case _: # Default case print("Unknown value") # Example usage: handle_value(42) # The answer to everything! handle_value(101) # Value is an integer greater than 100: 101 handle_value([1, 2]) # Parsed a list: 1, 2 handle_value("hello") # Unknown value ``` This example demonstrates using literal values (`42`), guard conditions with wildcards ( `_ as x if ...` ), sequence unpacking (`[a, b]`), and a default case (`_`).
